    15. Quadrupling Your Followers: What's So Special About Kat?
</h2>

  <!-- Attachment Blocks -->
<!-- DEBUG: attachments.count=14 -->

  <!-- DEBUG: attachment.id=69692651, kind=text -->
  <div class="lecture-attachment lecture-attachment-type-text" id="lecture-attachment-69692651">
    <div class="attachment-data"></div>
    
      <div class="attachment-data"></div>
<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><h1><strong><em>“I saw all these artists online that blew up after a few months of effort, and I just believed that I could be one of those artists.” Kat</em></strong></h1><p>Kat joined The Mystery School in December 2021. She had 800 followers and she wasn’t really selling tons of paintings yet.</p><p>By mid-February 2022, she was selling paintings regularly and had 4,542 followers.</p><p>By May 2022 she had 16k followers and was thriving in her new studio. (Instagram killed her account after she got hacked, unfortunately... <a href="https://www.tiktok.com/@art.stefanova.kat" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">But that hasn't hurt her Tiktok and her email list!</a>)</p><p>So, what’s so special about Kat?</p><p></p>
</div>

    
  </div>

  <!-- DEBUG: attachment.id=69692697, kind=text -->
  <div class="lecture-attachment lecture-attachment-type-text" id="lecture-attachment-69692697">
    <div class="attachment-data"></div>
    
      <div class="attachment-data"></div>
<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><p>She did it by sharing her story.</p><p>She started getting new fans the moment she let people in on what she was doing. They wanted to support her, so they bought her paintings.</p><p>Every post she shares has long detailed captions that document a part of her journey.</p><p>And Kat doesn't shy away from showing people what they want to see:</p><p>“The Artist At Work”.</p><p></p>
</div>

    
  </div>

  <!-- DEBUG: attachment.id=69692869, kind=text -->
  <div class="lecture-attachment lecture-attachment-type-text" id="lecture-attachment-69692869">
    <div class="attachment-data"></div>
    
      <div class="attachment-data"></div>
<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><p>Here is Kat's pivotal caption:</p><p><em>"Big News! I've left my part-time job so I can focus on my art full time. ✨🥳🎨 Change is scary, and to be honest I've spend a lot of time thinking about how being an artist is not a real job, how only a few can "make it"... At that time being an artist just didn't seem like a viable opportunity. 🤷‍♀️😅 It took me years to change. I started to look for examples. For people who choose to make art and it's working, they are succeeding I decided to work on my skills and insecurities and I slowly started to believe - it can be possible. The results of making this tiny effort, to change my belief, have been huge. A big milestone in my personal and artistic development. 👩‍🎨 I understand it may sound cliche, but believing is the single biggest reason for me being here, in my studio, writing this. Thanks you for being part of this journey, I hope it inspires you to be brave, question less and believe more. 💛☀️🙏"</em></p><p><strong>Wouldn't that kind of caption make YOU want to buy a painting or at least click to follow her journey?</strong></p><p>Her KPI (key performance indicator) isn't how many followers she has.</p><p>Her KPI is how many followers turn into customers. </p><p>REAL engagement and active participants is her goal, so she started talking to her tribe in the same way she would talk if she was updating real-life friends on what she’s been up to lately.</p><p></p>
</div>

  <div class="lecture-attachment lecture-attachment-type-text" id="lecture-attachment-69692959">

      <div class="attachment-data"></div>
<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><p>Kat only follows people that reinforce her Polaris Star.</p><p>She unfollowed everyone else.</p><p>Kat realized, looking at others who were less advanced than her (but killing it), that this game isn’t about “how good your art is.” Nope. There are other factors at play.</p><p>So she started studying those people.</p><p><em>“I’d see all these artists online that blew up after a few months of effort, and I just believed that I could be one of those artists.”</em></p><p></p>
</div>

  <div class="lecture-attachment lecture-attachment-type-text" id="lecture-attachment-69692949">

      <div class="attachment-data"></div>
<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><p>Kat turns every thought into a potential post.</p><p>She understands that this is part her job.</p><p>She understands that without all this "sharing with her audience" she wouldn’t be in the advantageous position she is in now.</p><p><em>“In the beginning it’s hard to figure out what to say. But it’s like everything else. It gets easier and easier.”</em></p><p>She found her cat sleeping on one of her paintings? Ok.... POST TO INSTAGRAM.</p><p>She's trying a new art technique? Ok... POST TO INSTAGRAM.</p><p>She's having trouble finishing painting? Ok... POST TO INSTAGRAM.</p><p></p>
</div>

    
  </div>

  <div class="lecture-attachment lecture-attachment-type-text" id="lecture-attachment-69692914">

      <div class="attachment-data"></div>
<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><p>You know how when you join The Mystery School, and I send you a module that I think would be perfect for your personal journey?</p><p>Well, when I found out what Kat was trying to do I sent her a link to this module:</p><p><a href="https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/enroll/lectures/22465060" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">HOW TO BUILD A CAREER IN 90 DAYS</a></p><p>2 Days later she announced to her followers that she was doing a 90-Day challenge.</p><p>She turned her journey into a game:</p><p>90 pieces of art in 90 days.</p><p><strong>***Take note MS students… Immediate execution with ideas that inspire you. If you don't move right away, you'll lose the momentum and energy of the moment.</strong></p><p></p>
</div>

<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><p>NOTE: Kat told me she was starting a newsletter when we spoke live. And the next day she sent me the landing page URL.</p><p><em>Once again... Immediate execution.</em></p><p>This is what she put on her landing page:</p><p>"Subscribe to join the art tribe. Sign up to receive the monthly art letter, access to discounts, and stay up-to-date with my creative journey."</p><p>"Art of email" checklist... Did she pass?</p><ol>
<li>Give us a clear reason we should subscribe? Check.</li>
<li>Tell us how often we’ll be getting emails? Check.</li>
</ol><p><br></p><p>===</p><p><br></p><h1><strong>My advice to Kat (that may help you too) </strong></h1><h2>1. Keep reminding people of your journey.</h2><p>Just because you told them once, doesn’t mean you can’t keep telling them every few weeks what your goal is. Some people need reminding. Some people never heard it the first time around.</p><p><br></p><h2>2. Sometimes you don’t need to make more art — you just need to make the art you ALREADY HAVE feel "priceless".</h2><p>Kat told me she has 20 pieces and she needs to make more so that there’s more variety. I don't think so. She needs to make those pieces more valuable. You do that by creating a story and a message around them. I explained that she needed to keep sharing her story and whatever shreds of message she’s figured out so far to make people buy her pieces REGARDLESS of what's left online. You raise the value of your work, not by creating more of it, but by creating a story around it that’s compelling. If they relate to the energy that you’re putting out into the world, then they will want to buy ANYTHING and EVERYTHING that you create. And you can get that aura around your work BEFORE the New York Times article... and BEFORE the celebrity endorsements. </p><p><strong>NOTE: By the time I put this module together, Kat had already made a new post sharing her story and goals again.</strong></p><p>What's so special about Kat?</p><p>You mean, besides her talent for painting?</p><p>And besides her masterful implementation of the color yellow?</p><p>It's this:</p><p><strong>SHE EXECUTES HER IDEAS IMMEDIATELY.</strong></p><p><em>Your turn.</em></p><p></p>
</div>
